description: This figure presents the mechanical properties of Fe-31Mn-9Al-1C-0.45Nb-0.45Mo-0.45W austenitic low-density steel subjected to different hot-rolling reductions (45%, 68%, and 82%), labeled as HR45, HR68, and HR82 respectively. Subfigure (a) displays room-temperature engineering stress-strain curves, where the blue curve (HR82) exhibits the highest ultimate tensile strength (~976 MPa) and yield strength (~648 MPa), while the gray curve (HR45) shows the lowest strength but highest ductility (~60% total elongation). Subfigure (b) illustrates Charpy V-notch impact energy decreasing linearly from 133±2.5 J/m² at 45% reduction to 105±1.2 J/m² at 82% reduction. The data demonstrate that increasing hot-rolling reduction enhances both yield strength (~170 MPa improvement) and ultimate tensile strength (~110 MPa improvement) through grain refinement and dislocation strengthening, with only minor reductions in uniform elongation (52% to 39%) and total elongation (60% to 50%). Despite the strength-ductility trade-off, all samples maintain excellent toughness above 100 J/m² and reduction of area exceeding 60%, indicating that the microstructural optimization via thermomechanical processing achieves superior mechanical property balance.
